Document:
# Cooking System
The Cooking system allows players to transform raw ingredients into delicious meals using heat sources and cooking devices.
## How It Works
Cooking requires a [bold]cooking device[/bold] (pot, oven, cauldron) and a [bold]heat source[/bold]. Place raw ingredients into the device and apply heat.
## Temperature System
Items transform based on the [bold]temperature[/bold] they reach:
[bold]Transformation Stages:[/bold] - [bold]Raw:[/bold] Original state of ingredients - [bold]Cooked:[/bold] Reaches optimal temperature (400K) — item is now edible - [bold]Burnt:[/bold] Exceeds safe temperature (500K+) — item becomes inedible ash
[bold]Temperature Ranges:[/bold] - 0-400K: Raw ingredients - 400-500K: Cooked (edible) - 500K+: Burnt (inedible, becomes trash)
## Examples
[bold]Dough Process:[/bold] 1. Place dough in cooking pot 2. Heat to 400-500K → transforms into [textlink="Bread" link="OE14CookingRecipes"] 3. If heated beyond 500K → becomes Ash (trash)
## Cooking Tips
- [bold]Watch the temperature[/bold] — too low won't cook, too high burns food - [bold]Different foods have different optimal temperatures[/bold] - [bold]Burnt food becomes trash[/bold] — watch closely! - [bold]Some recipes require specific ingredients[/bold]
See [textlink="Cooking Recipes" link="OE14CookingRecipes"] for a complete list of transformations.
## Related Systems
- [textlink="Crafting" link="OE14Crafting"] — workbench crafting - [textlink="Economy" link="OE14Economy"] — sell cooked food for profit
